nameMeanings.name: Linn
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: habitational name from a place called Linn, named with lin ‘swamp, bog, marsh’.
German: from an ancient Germanic personal name, Linto, formed with Old High German lind(i) ‘soft’.

variants.name: Lynn
meanings: Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Fhloinn and Ó Fhloinn (see Flynn ).

English: habitational name from any of several places so called in Norfolk, in particular King's Lynn, an important center of the medieval wool trade. The placename is probably from an Old Welsh word cognate with Gaelic linn ‘pool, stream’.

nameMeanings.name: Andrew
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the Greek name Andreas, a short form of any of various compound names derived from andr- ‘man, warrior’. In the New Testament this is the name of the first disciple to be called by Jesus. After the Resurrection, St Andrew preached in Asia Minor and Greece. He is traditionally believed to have been crucified at Patras in Achaia. He was one of the most popular saints of the Middle Ages and was adopted as the patron of Scotland, Russia, and Greece. It has long been among the most popular boys' names in the English-speaking world, especially in Scotland. Its popularity in England was further enhanced by its use as a British royal name for Prince Andrew ( b. 1960 ), the Duke of York.

nameMeanings.name: David
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, borne by the greatest of all the kings of Israel, whose history is recounted with great vividness in the first and second books of Samuel and elsewhere. As a boy he killed the giant Philistine Goliath with his slingshot. As king of Judah, and later of all Israel, he expanded the power of the Israelites and established the security of their kingdom. He was also noted as a poet, many of the Psalms being attributed to him. The Hebrew derivation of the name is uncertain; it is said by some to represent a nursery word meaning ‘darling’. It is a very popular Jewish name, but is almost equally common among Gentiles in the English-speaking world. It is particularly common in Wales and Scotland, having been borne by the patron saint of Wales (  see  Dewi   ) and by two medieval kings of Scotland.

nameMeanings.name: Cameron
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, which in the Highlands is derived from Gaelic cam sròn ‘crooked nose’, and in the Lowlands is apparently an assimilated form of a Norman baronial name derived from Cambernon in Normandy. It is very popular as a boy's name, and now also occurs as a girl's name .

variants.name: Kameron
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, which in the Highlands is derived from Gaelic cam sròn ‘crooked nose’, and in the Lowlands is apparently an assimilated form of a Norman baronial name derived from Cambernon in Normandy. It is very popular as a boy's name, and now also occurs as a girl's name .

variants.name: Camron
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, which in the Highlands is derived from Gaelic cam sròn ‘crooked nose’, and in the Lowlands is apparently an assimilated form of a Norman baronial name derived from Cambernon in Normandy. It is very popular as a boy's name, and now also occurs as a girl's name .

historicEvents.description: Great Catholic-Gaelic rebellion for return of lands, later joined by Old English Catholics in Ireland. Under leadership of Irish chieftain, Rory O'More, conspiracy was formed to seize Dublin and expel the English. English settlers were driven out of Ulster. Catholics hold 59% of land in Ireland.

The Protestant Lord Protector of England, Oliver Cromwell, landed at Dublin. His troops killed 2,000 men. A great part of lands in Munster, Leinster and Ulster (Drogheda and Wexford) was confiscated and divided among the English soldiers.
Over 6,000 Irish boys and women sold as slaves since England gained control of Jamaica.

en.lifeSummary: When Andrew David Cameron Linn was born in 1610, in Colorado, United States, his father, John Ellingsen Lynn, was 25 and his mother, Margaretha Van Croy Van Nieuwpoort, was 42. He had at least 2 sons with Ann Margaret Blair. He died on 6 August 1684, in County Waterford, Ireland, at the age of 74.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Andrew David Cameron Linn est né en 1610, à Colorado, États-Unis, son père, John Ellingsen Lynn, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Margaretha Van Croy Van Nieuwpoort, avait 42 ans. Il a eu au moins 2 fils avec Ann Margaret Blair. Il est décédé le 6 août 1684, à County Waterford, Irlande, à l’âge de 74 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Andrew David Cameron Linn 1610 in Colorado, Vereinigte Staaten geboren wurde, war sein Vater John Ellingsen Lynn 25 und seine Mutter Margaretha Van Croy Van Nieuwpoort 42. Er hatte mit Ann Margaret Blair mindestens 2 Söhne. Er starb am 6. August 1684 im Alter von 74 Jahren in County Waterford, Irland.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Andrew David Cameron Linn è nato il nel 1610 nel Colorado, Stati Uniti d'America, il suo padre, John Ellingsen Lynn, aveva 25 anni e il suo madre, Margaretha Van Croy Van Nieuwpoort, aveva 42 anni. Lui avuto almeno 2 figli con Ann Margaret Blair. Morì il 6 agosto 1684, nel County Waterford, Irlanda, all'età di 74 anni.
